CHELSEA JOIN REGUILON RACE

Chelsea have joined the race to sign Sergio Reguilon, who was voted “the best left back in La Liga” last season.

Last week Everton bid £18m for the Real Madrid academy product, who received rave reviews while on loan at Sevilla, helping the Andalusian club to fourth place and Champions League qualification.

The news may cast doubts over whether Chelsea will continue their interest in Leicester’s England left back Ben Chilwell. The West Londoners have so far failed to reach a deal, with both clubs poles apart on a valuation - although Leicester are believed to have softened their original demands for an £80m transfer fee.

For now, Chelsea have made their interest in Reguilon known – and are expected to trump Everton’s £18m bid in the coming days.

Manager Frank Lampard is keen to strengthen his defensive options with neither Marcos Alonso nor Emerson Palmieri managing to make the left back position their own last season.

Real Madrid rate their young star highly, but are blessed with talent in the left back position and drastically need to raise funds, a reported Euro 200m - to help balance the books. As well as the experienced campaigner Marcelo, now 32, Los Blancos have Frenchman Ferland Mendy who has enjoyed an impressive season at the Bernabeu.

Sevilla remain keen to take him on loan for another season, but are aware his performances have brought him to the attentions of major teams across Europe.
